what_it_does: Records a raised decision signal for the global visual-fidelity decision, stating the chosen option and that gated work may resume.
observations: It is a markdown ledger item with YAML frontmatter under company/channel-memory/noticeboard.
The item type is 'signal' and state is 'raised'.
It links to decision://global/visual-fidelity via an attached_to relationship.
The chosen option is 'Push the drawn style further first'.
It notes that work gated on the decision can RESUME, but the resume step remains the lane's/operator's gated step.
standouts: The file explicitly gates downstream work on a decision URI (decision://global/visual-fidelity).
It distinguishes the decision signal from the actual resume action.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
Follows a board:// URI addressing scheme and decision:// URI for linked decisions.
Includes a human-readable body after the frontmatter summarizing the signal.
concerns: The 'channel' and 'thread' fields are empty; their intended use is not defined in this file.
The body repeats the frontmatter content, which could drift if not kept in sync.
notes: A raised signal that the visual-fidelity decision is settled and the 'drawn style' option is selected, unblocking gated work.
questions: What does 'lane's/operator's gated step' refer to in the workflow?
What process or actor consumes this signal to actually resume work?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record a decision signal and unblock gated work, which matches the file content.
intention_signals: It is trying to communicate that a pending design decision has been resolved with a specific option, so downstream tasks waiting on that decision can proceed.
novelty: It treats a decision as a first-class addressable entity (decision://global/visual-fidelity) and the signal as a separate, resumable event.
contribution: Provides a durable, timestamped record of a resolved decision option and its unblocking effect on gated work, enabling workflow state machines or queries to locate resumable tasks.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal recording the global visual-fidelity decision's chosen option 'Push the drawn style further first' and indicating that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To notify downstream work that the visual-fidelity decision is decided and the drawn-style option is selected, allowing gated tasks to resume.
